name: Professor Daniela Gabor
title: Professor of Economics
biography: She studies central banks, shadow money, just transitions and green industrial policies through a critical macrofinance lens. Daniela has served as an expert advisor for the European Parliament, the G20 under the Brazil Presidency, the United Nations 4th Financing for Development Agenda, civil society organisations and central banks. She is currently running two funded projects, REDCAJU (Rethinking Developmentalism for Climate and Social Justice) with Ndongo Samba Sylla, IDEAS Network Africa, and REDEF (Redesigning Finance for Climate Justice – a Big Green State approach). At SOAS, she is teaching International Finance, Macroecnomics and Global Economic Policy Analysis. She is working on a book on the Wall Street Consensus for Norton.
research_interests: Critical macrofinance; Institutional capital; Derisking state; Monetary theory; Decarbonisation
